events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check for VFDs & motor controls service call. Scenario: field-service visit to installed operating asset, observed field condition: wrong overload class setting. Work actively in progress. Reference NEC 430, mfr manuals. Need decide decision pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since wrong overload class setting is non-compliant and potentially dangerous (motor overload protection mis-coordination), decision fail. Risk high or critical? Wrong overload class can cause nuisance trips or failure to protect motor, fire hazard. Critical maybe because installed operating asset, live? But not necessarily immediate shock. High. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60? Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Order: not audio task, maybe empty or sequence IDs? The schema says order list of step-id. Since task is sequence check, we should identify order violation. But no specific steps provided. We can list expected correct order: verify lockout, consult manual, check settings, correct overload class, test/verify, document. But schema order is step-id strings. We can provide generic IDs. Workable empty? The task asks what must happen before work can continue. Actions: immediate corrective action: de-energize VFD/control circuit per LOTO, verify motor FLA/nameplate/service factor, set overload class per NEC 430 and manufacturer, verify/test, restore. Findings: overload class misconfigured; potential motor burnout/fire; non-compliant with NEC 430. Rationale. References NEC 430 Part III (overload protection), mfr manuals. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [
    "verify-loto-de-energize",
    "review-nameplate-fla-manual",
    "select-correct-overload-class",
    "program-adjust-setting",
    "verify-test-function",
    "restore-document"
  ],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Overload class setting is incorrect for the installed motor/application",
    "Mis-setting can result in inadequate motor overload protection or nuisance tripping",
    "Condition is non-compliant with NEC 430 overload protection requirements and OEM instructions"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt work and secure the circuit under proper LOTO before adjusting settings",
    "Verify motor nameplate FLA, service factor, and application requirements",
    "Set overload class per NEC 430 and the VFD/motor manufacturer manual",
    "Test/verify protection function and record final settings before returning to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "An incorrect overload class setting on an operating VFD/motor control is a code and safety defect. Work cannot continue or be accepted until the setting is corrected and verified per NEC 430 and the OEM manual.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 430 - Motor Circuits, Overload Protection",
    "VFD/motor manufacturer service manuals"
  ]
}
